Joshua allotted inheritances to Judah, Ephraim, and half of Manasseh before they left Gilgal. Afterwards, when they moved to Shiloh, another survey was made, and the other tribes had their portions assigned. In due time all God's people were settled.

Achsah obtained some land by Caleb’s free grant. He gave her a south region. It was indeed land, but a south region, dry and prone to be parched. She requested more, and he gave the upper and lower springs. Those who understand it as one field, watered both by rain from heaven and springs from the earth, support the common allusion made when we pray for spiritual and heavenly blessings that relate to our souls, as blessings of the upper springs, and those which relate to the body and the life we now have, as blessings of the lower springs. All blessings, both of the upper and lower springs, belong to God’s children. As related to Christ, they have them freely given by the Father, as part of their inheritance.

Here is a list of the cities of Judah. We do not find Bethlehem here, which later became the city of David and was ennobled by the birth of our Lord Jesus in it. That city, even at its best, was small among the thousands of Judah, except that it was honored this way; now it was so small as to not be considered one of the cities.
